projectDescription: A TypeScript library for detecting synchronized cryptocurrency pump signals from Telegram channels, with author-cluster deduplication, path-aware exit replay, liquidation-cascade detection, and a five-barrier statistical certification system to prevent brute-force overfitting.
keyFeatures: Author-cluster deduplication via union-find to distinguish real capital inflow from single-actor multi-channel manipulation
Path-aware exit replay on 1m OHLC candles — labels training data from your actual prod exit, not close-to-close
Liquidation-cascade detection (symmetric long/short): volZ + squeezePressure with configurable policy (none / tighten / veto / invert)
Five-barrier statistical certification (DSR, PBO, SPA, minTRL, nested-CV OOS) to reject brute-force artifacts
Exit tensor [mode][channel][symbol][direction][volRegime] with hierarchical fallback — each source/asset/direction gets its own trained exit
Meta-overfitting guard (MetaLedger): cadence gate + family-wise DSR correction across repeated fit() attempts
One-standard-error winner selection (Breiman) over time-series K-fold CV to prefer robust configurations over lucky outliers
Stationarity window — rolling author-matrix over a configurable recent window to avoid regime drift on long histories
Per-asset tuned grids for 15 cryptocurrencies (BTC to Fartcoin) in config/
538 tests across 52 test files including e2e noise-rejection and adversarial statistics tests
publicApiSurface: model.planFor(symbol, dir, channel, candles, policy?) → TradeSignal | null
model.planForAt(symbol, dir, channel, candles, ts, policy?) → BacktestSignal | null
